message:
feat(model): add content_hash/external_ref fields and split closed vs tombstone status predicates

Add two new optional fields to the Issue model:
- `content_hash: Option<String>` — an internal content hash for dedup,
  computed at runtime and excluded from JSON serialization via
  `skip_serializing`. This supports future deduplication workflows
  without breaking the wire format.
- `external_ref: Option<String>` — an optional link to an external issue
  tracker (e.g., a GitHub issue URL), serialized only when present via
  `skip_serializing_if`.

Introduce fine-grained status predicates on Issue:
- `is_closed()` returns true only for "closed" status (not tombstone).
- `is_tombstone()` returns true only for "tombstone" status.
- The existing `is_closed_like()` continues to match both terminal states.

This distinction matters because tombstoned issues are permanently removed
(should never resurface in triage) while closed issues may still be
relevant for velocity metrics and historical analysis. The diff engine
in `analysis/diff.rs` now tracks tombstones separately in
`SnapshotCounts`, using a new `terminal()` helper to combine both for
the closed-issues metric delta. The `snapshot_counts` function now uses
the model predicates instead of manual string matching, and properly
accounts for each status branch (tombstone vs closed vs open/blocked).

The `alerts.rs` test helper is updated with the new default fields.

Tests added:
- `is_closed_vs_tombstone_distinction` — verifies the three predicates
  across closed, tombstone, and open issues.
- `content_hash_and_external_ref_defaults` — confirms None defaults and
  round-trip behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
